reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Excellent professor! Explains complex algorithms in a way that's easy to understand. Very approachable during office hours and genuinely cares about student success.

reviews.subject: Mathematics
reviews.review: Great at breaking down calculus concepts. Homework can be challenging but prepares you well for exams. Always willing to help if you put in the effort.

reviews.subject: Physics
reviews.review: Lectures can be dry but the material is well-organized. Tests are fair if you study the textbook. Could improve on making the class more engaging.

reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Best data structures professor I've had. Makes trees, graphs, and dynamic programming feel intuitive. His coding examples in class are incredibly helpful.

reviews.subject: English
reviews.review: Passionate about literature and it shows. Gives detailed feedback on essays. Class discussions are thought-provoking and she values every student's perspective.

reviews.subject: Chemistry
reviews.review: Moves too fast through material. Labs are poorly organized and the grading is inconsistent. Would recommend taking this course with a different professor.

reviews.subject: Psychology
reviews.review: Engaging lecturer who uses real-world case studies. Exams are straightforward if you attend lectures. One of the best professors in the psychology department.

reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Solid software engineering course. Teaches practical skills like Git, CI/CD, and agile methodology. Group projects simulate real work environments well.

reviews.subject: Biology
reviews.review: Content is thorough but the pace is fast. Study groups are essential for this class. Extra credit opportunities help balance tough exam scores.

reviews.subject: History
reviews.review: Makes history come alive with storytelling. No boring PowerPoints—every lecture feels like a documentary. Fair grading and flexible with deadlines when needed.

reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Strong AI and machine learning course. Challenging but rewarding. Professor Liu stays current with industry trends and brings real research into the classroom.

reviews.subject: Economics
reviews.review: Reads directly from slides and doesn't explain concepts in depth. Office hours are rarely available. Textbook is essentially required to pass.

reviews.subject: Mathematics
reviews.review: Patient and thorough. Breaks down linear algebra into manageable pieces. Always available for extra help and creates practice exams that mirror the real thing.

reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Knows the material well but struggles with explaining it clearly. Database course covers good content but lectures can be confusing. TA sessions helped more.

reviews.subject: Philosophy
reviews.review: Thought-provoking discussions every class. Grades fairly on papers and values original thinking over memorization. Highly recommend for anyone interested in ethics.

reviews.subject: Electrical Engineering
reviews.review: Challenging circuits course but extremely rewarding. Lab work is well-designed and the professor is always willing to stay late to help debug circuits.

reviews.subject: Statistics
reviews.review: Makes statistics fun—which I never thought was possible. Uses real datasets and Python examples. Best professor for learning data analysis fundamentals.

reviews.subject: Political Science
reviews.review: Interesting topics but the workload is heavy. Weekly essays and mandatory participation. Good if you want to improve your writing but expect to work hard.

reviews.subject: Computer Science
reviews.review: Outstanding web development course. Covers React, Next.js, and deployment. Projects are portfolio-worthy and she provides excellent code reviews.

reviews.subject: Mechanical Engineering
reviews.review: Solid thermodynamics course. Clear explanations with lots of worked examples. Exams are tough but fair. Drops the lowest homework grade which is nice.
